title: National Scholarship Portal (NSP)
shortDescription: A one-stop digital platform for various government scholarships ranging from pre-matric to post-graduate levels.
eligibility: Students from minority, SC/ST, and OBC communities, as well as general category students meeting specific income criteria.

fees.details: No application fees are charged for submitting scholarship forms.

stepsToApply: Visit the official National Scholarship Portal and click on the 'New Registration' tab located in the Applicant Corner.
Read the detailed undertaking guidelines carefully, check the mandatory agreement boxes, and click on 'Continue'.
Fill in the initial registration form with your State of Domicile, Scholarship Category (Pre/Post Matric), Name, DOB, and Aadhaar number.
Verify your registered mobile number using the One-Time Password (OTP) received to generate your unique Application ID and password.

Log in using the newly generated credentials, complete the detailed academic and domiciliary application form, upload all required scanned documents, and submit the form for institutional verification.

title: National Career Service (NCS)
shortDescription: A national portal connecting job seekers with employers, providing career counseling, and vocational training resources.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen seeking employment, skill development, or career guidance.

fees.details: Completely free registration and usage for job seekers.

requiredDocuments: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, or Voter ID
stepsToApply: Navigate to the NCS official portal homepage and click on the 'Jobseeker' tab under the 'Register' section.
Select your Unique Identification Type (Aadhaar, UAN, eShram, or PAN) and enter the corresponding unique identification number.
Fill out the comprehensive profile form including your educational qualifications, specialized skill sets, work experience, and preferred job locations.
Verify your mobile number and email address through OTP to activate your official NCS ID.
Log in to your personalized dashboard to search for active job postings, apply directly to employers, or book free career counseling sessions.

ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
title: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
shortDescription: Flagship scheme providing industry-relevant skill training to Indian youth to help them secure better livelihoods.
eligibility: School/college dropouts or unemployed youth of Indian nationality holding an Aadhaar card and a bank account.

fees.details: Training and assessment fees are entirely paid by the Government.

stepsToApply: Visit the Skill India Digital portal or the PMKVY official website and click on 'Find a Training Centre'.
Use the search filters to locate an authorized PMKVY training center near your location based on your preferred sector and job role.
Visit the selected training center physically with your Aadhaar card, bank passbook, and photographs.
Complete the counseling session with the center coordinator to determine the best-fit course based on your aptitude.
Fill out the enrollment form provided by the center, complete your biometric registration, and commence your daily training classes.

title: SWAYAM - Free Online Courses
shortDescription: National MOOCs portal providing free, high-quality online certification courses taught by top Indian university professors.
eligibility: Open to anyone worldwide, regardless of age, qualification, or geographical location.

fees.details: Learning is entirely free. An optional fee (approx. ₹1000) applies only if an official proctored exam certificate is desired.

requiredDocuments: No documents required for learning. Aadhaar/Photo ID needed only if registering for the proctored exam.
stepsToApply: Access the SWAYAM portal and click the 'Sign In / Register' button at the top right corner.
Create an account using your Microsoft, Google, or Facebook credentials, or sign up with a dedicated email address.
Browse the extensive course catalog by filtering through categories such as Engineering, Humanities, or Management.
Select your desired course, check the enrollment timeline, and click the 'Join' button to add it to your dashboard.
To obtain a verified certificate, navigate to the course announcements near the end of the term, click the 'Register for Exam' link, pay the examination fee, and select a test center.

title: Vidya Lakshmi Education Loan Portal
shortDescription: A single-window platform for students to apply for educational loans and government subsidies across multiple banks.
eligibility: Indian students seeking to pursue higher education in recognized institutions in India or abroad.

fees.details: Portal usage is free. Standard bank loan processing fees may apply upon loan sanction.

requiredDocuments: KYC Documents of Student and Co-applicant
stepsToApply: Visit the Vidya Lakshmi portal and click on 'Register' to create a new user profile using your basic details and email address.
Log in to your account and click on 'Loan Application Form' (CELAF - Common Educational Loan Application Form).
Fill out the multi-tab CELAF form meticulously, detailing your personal information, academic background, course details, and precise financial requirements.
Navigate to the 'Search for Loan Scheme' section, filter schemes based on your course and loan amount, and select up to three different banks/schemes.
Submit the application digitally, check your dashboard regularly for status updates from the banks, and upload any additional query documents the bank manager requests.

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
title: UPSC One Time Registration (OTR)
shortDescription: Mandatory centralized registration profile for candidates applying to civil services and other UPSC examinations.
eligibility: Indian citizens planning to appear for UPSC examinations (must meet specific age and qualification criteria per exam).

fees.details: Creating the OTR profile is free. Examination specific fees apply later during application.

requiredDocuments: Valid Mobile Number and Email ID
Class 10th Board Roll Number and Marksheet
Valid Photo ID (Aadhaar, Voter ID, PAN)
stepsToApply: Visit the UPSC Online portal and click on the prominent 'One Time Registration (OTR) for Examinations' button.
Click on 'New Registration' and fill in your fundamental details exactly as they appear on your Class 10th certificate (Name, Father's Name, DOB).
Submit the form to receive OTPs on both your registered mobile number and email ID for dual verification.
Log in using the newly created OTR ID and set a strong, secure password for future portal access.
Whenever a new UPSC notification is released, log in using this OTR profile to apply instantly, as your demographic data will be pre-filled automatically.

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
title: SSC Online Registration Portal
shortDescription: Official platform to register and apply for various Group B and C government posts like CGL, CHSL, and MTS.
eligibility: Varies by exam (10th pass for MTS, 12th pass for CHSL, Graduates for CGL); generally ages 18-32.

fees.details: Registration is free. Exam fee is ₹100 (Exempt for Women/SC/ST/PwD/Ex-Servicemen).

stepsToApply: Open the new SSC official portal (ssc.gov.in) and click on the 'Login or Register' button, then select 'Register Now'.
Complete the basic details section by providing your Aadhaar number, name, DOB, and matriculation board details for initial verification.
Verify your contact details using OTP and save the generated Registration Number and system-assigned password.
Log in again, change your password as prompted, and proceed to fill in additional details like category, nationality, and permanent address.
Read the final declaration, click 'I Agree', submit the OTR, and then navigate to the 'Latest Notifications' tab to apply for active exam cycles.

title: Academic Bank of Credits (ABC)
shortDescription: A digital repository that holds academic credits earned by students, facilitating credit transfer and multiple entry/exit options.
eligibility: Students enrolled in recognized higher education institutions in India.

fees.details: Account creation and credit storage are completely free.

requiredDocuments: Aadhaar Card (linked to a mobile number)
stepsToApply: Visit the Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) website and click on 'My Account' followed by 'Student'.
You will be redirected to the DigiLocker MeriPehchaan portal; sign in using your existing DigiLocker mobile number and PIN.
If you do not have a DigiLocker account, click 'Sign Up', verify your Aadhaar via OTP, and create a security PIN.
Once logged in, provide consent for ABC to access your DigiLocker profile data.
Your unique 12-digit ABC ID will be generated instantly. Note this ID and share it with your college/university to ensure your academic credits are securely deposited into your digital account.

title: National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS)
shortDescription: Skill training scheme equipping fresh engineering/diploma graduates with practical workplace knowledge and a monthly stipend.
eligibility: Indian nationals who have passed a Degree/Diploma in Engineering or general streams within the last 3 to 5 years.

fees.details: Zero registration fee for students.

stepsToApply: Visit the NATS 2.0 portal and click on the 'Student Registration' button prominently displayed on the homepage.
Complete the eligibility check questionnaire by answering questions about your graduation year, mode of study, and previous training experience.
Fill in the detailed enrollment form by providing your personal, educational, and bank account information required for direct benefit transfers.
Upload clear scanned copies of your qualifying degree/provisional certificate, photograph, and signature in the prescribed file size formats.
Submit the application to receive a 16-digit student enrollment number; once verified by the board, you can log in and apply directly to establishment vacancies.

ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
title: National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS)
shortDescription: Promotes apprenticeship training across various trades by providing financial support to establishments employing apprentices.
eligibility: Minimum 14 years of age; educational qualification varies from 5th class pass to graduation depending on the designated trade.

fees.details: No application charges for candidates.

stepsToApply: Access the Apprenticeship India portal and click on the 'Login/Register' dropdown, then select 'Candidate'.
Choose 'Register as a Candidate', input your mobile number and email ID, and verify them using the system-generated OTPs.
Log in to your new account and click on 'Complete Your Profile' to enter basic details, disability status, and educational qualifications.
Navigate to the 'e-KYC' section to authenticate your profile using your Aadhaar number and biometrics/OTP.
Update your bank account details for stipend processing, then use the 'Apprenticeship Opportunities' tab to search and apply for openings using your verified profile.

title: Central Teacher Eligibility Test (CTET)
shortDescription: National level mandatory qualifying examination for candidates aspiring to be teachers for classes I to VIII in central government schools.
eligibility: Candidates with Senior Secondary plus D.El.Ed (for Paper I) or Graduation plus B.Ed (for Paper II).

fees.details: ₹1000 for one paper / ₹1200 for both (General/OBC); ₹500/₹600 for SC/ST/PwD.

stepsToApply: Visit the official CTET website during the active notification window and click on the 'Apply for CTET' link.
Click on 'New Registration', read the comprehensive information bulletin, check the declaration box, and proceed.
Fill in the online application form with personal details, choose your examination centers, and select the language and paper (Paper I, II, or both) you wish to appear for.
Upload the scanned images of your latest photograph and signature strictly adhering to the specified dimension and size limits.
Proceed to the payment gateway, remit the examination fee using a Debit/Credit Card or Net Banking, and print the final confirmation page for future reference.

title: JEE Main Examination Registration
shortDescription: Standardized computer-based test for admission to various undergraduate engineering and architecture programs (NITs, IIITs, etc.).
eligibility: Candidates who have passed Class 12 (or equivalent) with PCM in the last two years or are appearing in the current year.

fees.details: Approx. ₹1000 for Male General/OBC, ₹800 for Female/SC/ST/PwD per session.

requiredDocuments: Scanned Photograph, Signature, and Left-hand Thumb Impression
stepsToApply: Access the JEE Main NTA portal and select the 'JEE (Main) Session Registration' active link.
Create an account via DigiLocker, ABC ID, or Aadhaar authentication to generate your Application Number and Password.
Log in and complete the application form, entering personal details, choice of paper (B.E./B.Tech, B.Arch), and your four preferred examination cities.
Upload the required scanned documents ensuring they meet the exact NTA size and format guidelines (usually JPEG/JPG).
Pay the examination fee via the integrated payment gateway, verify the transaction status, and safely download the digitally generated confirmation page.

title: NEET (UG) Registration
shortDescription: Single national level entrance examination for admission to MBBS, BDS, and AYUSH medical courses in India.
eligibility: Candidates aged 17 or above having passed Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotechnology, and English.

fees.details: ₹1700 (General), ₹1600 (General-EWS/OBC-NCL), ₹1000 (SC/ST/PwD/Third Gender).

requiredDocuments: Signature and Left & Right hand fingers/thumb impressions
stepsToApply: Visit the official NTA NEET UG portal and click on 'New Candidate Registration' during the application window.
Read the instructions, accept the declaration, and register using Aadhaar, DigiLocker, or Passport details to obtain an Application Number.
Fill out the comprehensive application detailing your academic records, medium of question paper, and preferred exam city centers.
Upload all required media, specifically paying attention to the strict formatting rules for the postcard-size photograph and the 10-finger impressions.
Complete the final fee payment online, verify the 'Success' status, and download the full application PDF to present during counseling.

title: UGC NET Examination
shortDescription: Test to determine the eligibility of Indian nationals for 'Assistant Professor' and 'Junior Research Fellowship' in Indian universities.
eligibility: Candidates who have secured at least 55% marks (without rounding off) in their Master's Degree or equivalent.

fees.details: ₹1150 (General), ₹600 (OBC-NCL/EWS), ₹325 (SC/ST/PwD/Third Gender).

stepsToApply: Navigate to the NTA UGC NET website and click the portal link for online registration.
Register using your basic identity details to generate an application number, which will be sent via SMS and email.
Log in and fill out the detailed form, carefully selecting the subject you wish to appear for (must relate to your PG degree) and the exam center preferences.
Upload your scanned recent passport-size photograph and your signature in JPG/JPEG format.
Pay the requisite application fee securely through SBI/HDFC/Syndicate/ICICI/Paytm payment gateways and retain the printed confirmation slip.

department: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
title: AICTE Pragati Scholarship for Girls
shortDescription: A scheme aimed at providing assistance to girl students pursuing technical education (Degree/Diploma) from AICTE approved institutions.
eligibility: Girl students admitted to 1st year of Degree/Diploma or 2nd year (lateral entry) in AICTE approved institutions; family income < ₹8 Lakhs.

fees.details: No application fees.

stepsToApply: Go to the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) website, as AICTE routes its scholarship applications through this centralized platform.
Register as a new user or log in with your existing NSP credentials.
Search for the 'AICTE Pragati Scholarship Scheme for Girl Students' under the technical or central schemes section.
Fill in the application data accurately, ensuring the institution name and course details match the AICTE approved database.
Upload the required family income certificates and admission proofs, then submit the application to your college nodal officer for phase-one verification.

title: DIKSHA (PM e-VIDYA)
shortDescription: National digital infrastructure for teachers and students offering digitized textbooks, interactive learning materials, and video lessons.
eligibility: Open to all students, teachers, and parents across India.

fees.details: Accessing digital content and textbooks is completely free.

requiredDocuments: No documents required for basic access. School ID required if enrolling in specific state training modules.
stepsToApply: Visit the DIKSHA portal website or download the DIKSHA mobile application from the Google Play Store or Apple App Store.
Upon opening the app, select your primary role (Teacher, Student, or Parent) to customize the user interface.
Select your relevant State Board, medium of instruction (Language), and specific Class grades to filter the content library.
Scan the QR codes printed on physical NCERT or state board textbooks using the app's inbuilt scanner to instantly load interactive digital supplements.
Optionally, create a free user profile to track your learning progress, save downloaded courses, and earn completion certificates.

ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
title: Skill India Digital (SID)
shortDescription: State-of-the-art digital platform bringing all skill development initiatives, jobs, and entrepreneurial support under one unified portal.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen looking to upgrade their skills or seek employment.

fees.details: Platform registration is free. Specific advanced premium courses may carry fees.

stepsToApply: Open the Skill India Digital website or application and click on 'Register'.
Enter your active 10-digit mobile number and authenticate it using the OTP sent to your device.
Set a secure 4-digit security PIN for quick future logins.
Complete your e-KYC by entering your Aadhaar number; this verifies your identity and instantly generates a Skill India Digital ID and QR code.
Navigate to the 'Skill Courses' or 'Job Exchange' sections, update your digital CV, and start enrolling in bite-sized online courses or applying for jobs.

department: National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT)
title: National Talent Search Examination (NTSE)
shortDescription: National level scholarship program to identify and nurture talented students studying in Class X, providing financial assistance up to Ph.D.
eligibility: Students currently studying in Class X in recognized schools across India.

fees.details: State level fees vary by state (often free or nominal). NCERT does not charge for the Stage II exam.

requiredDocuments: Caste/Disability Certificate (if applying under reserved quotas)
stepsToApply: Check the specific notification released by your respective State/UT education board (SCERT) for Stage I of the NTSE.
Download the application form from the state board website or collect a physical copy from your school's principal/nodal teacher.
Fill out the form completely, attach two recent passport photographs, and submit it to your school authorities along with the requisite state fee.
The school principal will verify the credentials and forward the bulk applications to the State Liaison Officer before the deadline.
If you clear the Stage I state-level examination, your details will be automatically forwarded to NCERT, and you will be issued a roll number to appear for the national level Stage II exam.

title: Vidyanjali (School Volunteer Programme)
shortDescription: Initiative connecting volunteers, professionals, and organizations with government schools to enhance the quality of education.
eligibility: Any individual professional, retired personnel, NGO, or corporate entity willing to volunteer time or assets.

fees.details: Completely free voluntary service platform.

stepsToApply: Go to the Vidyanjali web portal and click on 'Login / Register' on the top menu bar.
Select 'Volunteer Registration' and choose whether you are registering as an Individual, NRI, NGO, or Organization.
Fill in your personal and contact details, and verify your mobile number and email using OTPs.
Once logged in, browse through the 'Service/Activity Requests' or 'Asset/Material Requests' posted by various government schools nationwide.
Select a request that matches your expertise or capability, click 'Participate', and wait for the school administration to review and approve your connection.

title: e-Shram Portal
shortDescription: National database for unorganized workers providing them with a Unique Universal Account Number (UAN) for social security and job-matching.
eligibility: Any unorganized sector worker (construction, gig worker, street vendor, domestic helper) aged between 16 and 59, not an EPFO/ESIC member.

fees.details: Self-registration is free. Registration through CSCs is also free (CSCs are paid by the government).

requiredDocuments: Savings Bank Account Number with IFSC
stepsToApply: Visit the official e-Shram portal and click on 'Register on e-Shram' located on the homepage.
Enter your Aadhaar-linked mobile number and the visual captcha code, declare that you are not a member of EPFO/ESIC, and click 'Send OTP'.
Input the OTP received and proceed to enter your 12-digit Aadhaar number for biometric or OTP-based authentication.
Carefully fill in the subsequent digital forms detailing your personal information, address, educational qualification, primary occupation (using the provided NCO codes), and bank account details.
Preview the finalized form, submit it, and immediately download your generated digital e-Shram card featuring your 12-digit UAN.

title: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education
shortDescription: Scholarship program to attract talented youth to study basic and natural sciences at the undergraduate and postgraduate levels.
eligibility: Students who happen to be among the top 1% in 12th standard at their respective Board Examinations and are pursuing courses in Natural and Basic Sciences.

fees.details: No application fees.

stepsToApply: Visit the online INSPIRE web portal and click on 'Register Here' to create a new user account.
Log in with your newly created credentials and navigate to the 'Scholarship' section.
Fill in the detailed application form, providing your educational background, board rank, and college enrollment details.
Upload scanned copies of your Class 12 marksheet, the official Endorsement Certificate signed by your college principal, and your bank details.
Submit the application digitally and wait for the DST to release the list of conditionally accepted candidates based on the board cut-offs.

title: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMS)
shortDescription: Financial assistance awarded to meritorious students of economically weaker sections to arrest their drop-out at class VIII.
eligibility: Students studying in Class VIII in government/aided schools with family income less than ₹3,50,000 per annum.

fees.details: State-level exam fees may range from ₹0 to ₹100 depending on the state.

stepsToApply: Contact your school principal to confirm the state-level NMMS selection test notification dates.
Fill out the state-level examination form (online or offline as per state rules) and appear for the MAT and SAT tests.
Upon clearing the exam and being selected in the state merit list, go to the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
Register as a new user, select the NMMS scheme, and fill out the detailed digital application form.
Submit the form for institutional verification by your school nodal officer to initiate direct bank transfers.

title: NPTEL Online Certification
shortDescription: Provides high-quality recorded engineering, science, and humanities courses taught by IIT and IISc professors.
eligibility: Open to all students, professionals, and lifelong learners regardless of educational background.

fees.details: Learning is free. ₹1000 per course is charged only for the optional proctored certification exam.

requiredDocuments: Recent Photograph and Signature (for exam registration)
College ID or Aadhaar (for exam day verification)
stepsToApply: Visit the Swayam NPTEL portal and log in using your Google or Microsoft account.
Browse the current semester's course catalog and click 'Join' on the courses you wish to study.
Watch the weekly video lectures and complete the mandatory weekly assignments before the deadline.
When the exam registration window opens, fill out the exam form, choose your preferred test city, and pay the ₹1000 fee.
Download your hall ticket, appear for the in-person proctored exam, and receive an e-certificate featuring IIT logos upon passing.

title: GATE (Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering)
shortDescription: National examination testing the comprehensive understanding of undergraduate engineering and science subjects for PG admissions and PSU jobs.
eligibility: Candidates currently in the 3rd or higher years of any undergraduate degree program, or who have already completed any government-approved degree.

fees.details: ₹900 for Female/SC/ST/PwD; ₹1800 for all other candidates per paper.

stepsToApply: Access the GATE Online Application Processing System (GOAPS) portal during the active registration window.
Register as a new user by providing your name, email ID, mobile number, and a secure password.
Log in and complete the application by entering your personal details, academic qualifications, and selecting up to two test papers.
Upload high-quality scanned images of your photograph, signature, and category certificates strictly matching the required dimensions.
Pay the application fee online, review the final submitted form, and print the confirmation page.

title: CUET (UG) Registration
shortDescription: Common University Entrance Test providing a single-window opportunity to students to seek admission in participating Central Universities.
eligibility: Candidates who have passed the Class 12 / equivalent examination or are appearing in the current year.

fees.details: Varies based on the number of subjects chosen and candidate category (approx. ₹750 to ₹1750).

requiredDocuments: Active Email ID and Mobile Number
stepsToApply: Visit the official CUET UG Samarth portal and click on 'Register'.
Download and read the Information Bulletin carefully before accepting the declaration to proceed.
Fill in the online registration form using personal details to generate an Application Number and create a password.
Log in to complete the application form, carefully selecting the universities and corresponding subject domains you wish to test for.
Upload the required document scans, pay the calculated examination fee via the online gateway, and save the confirmation page.

title: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
shortDescription: Placement-linked skill training program specifically targeted at rural poor youth to provide them with regular minimum wage jobs.
eligibility: Rural youth from poor families in the age group of 15 to 35 years (upper age limit extended to 45 for women/SC/ST/PwD).

fees.details: Completely free of cost, including training, accommodation, and food.

requiredDocuments: BPL Card or Antyodaya Anna Yojana Card
Panchayat Certification of Rural Poor Status
stepsToApply: Visit the official Kaushal Panjee website (kaushalpanjee.nic.in) or download the Kaushal Panjee app.
Register yourself as a candidate by entering your mobile number and Aadhaar details.
Fill in your socio-economic details, educational background, and preferred employment sector.
Attend the local mobilization camp organized by the Gram Panchayat or Project Implementing Agencies (PIAs) in your district.
Undergo counseling and an aptitude test at the center, enroll in the designated batch, and commence your free residential training.

ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
title: PM-DAKSH Yojana Skill Training
shortDescription: National action plan for skilling marginalized persons covering SCs, OBCs, EBCs, De-notified tribes, and sanitation workers.
eligibility: Target groups (SC/OBC/EBC/Safai Karamcharis) aged 18-45 years with specific income limits for OBC/EBCs.

fees.details: Free training with stipends (₹1000 to ₹1500 per month) during the course duration.

stepsToApply: Visit the PM-DAKSH portal or download the PM-DAKSH mobile application.
Click on 'Candidate Registration' and authenticate using your Aadhaar-linked mobile number via OTP.
Fill out the registration form with personal details, category verification, and educational qualifications.
Search for active training centers near you using the portal's geographic filter and apply for a specific job role.
Visit the selected training center with original documents for physical verification to finalize your batch enrollment.

title: IGNOU Online Admission (Samarth Portal)
shortDescription: Centralized admission portal for enrolling in distance and online degree, diploma, and certificate programs offered by IGNOU.
eligibility: Varies extensively by program (10+2 for UG, Graduation for PG, 10th pass for basic certificates).

fees.details: Course fees range from ₹2000 to ₹25000+ per year depending on the program. Registration fee is ₹300.

requiredDocuments: Category Certificate (for fee exemption if applicable)
stepsToApply: Access the IGNOU Samarth Admission Portal and click on the 'Click Here for New Registration' button.
Create a user account by providing your name, email, mobile number, and setting a password.
Log in and fill out the detailed profile, selecting your desired program type (ODL or Online) and specific course.
Select your preferred Regional Center and Study Center for administrative support and examinations.
Upload all required scanned academic documents, pay the program fee securely via the payment gateway, and download the admission form.

title: Join Indian Army (Agnipath & Regular)
shortDescription: Official portal for recruitment of Officers and Agniveers (Other Ranks) into the Indian Army.
eligibility: Agniveer: Ages 17.5 to 21 years (10th/12th pass). Officers: Ages 16.5 to 27+ depending on entry scheme (NDA/CDS/Tech).

fees.details: ₹250 examination fee for Agniveer CE E. Officer entry fees vary (usually ₹200 for UPSC entries).

stepsToApply: Visit the Join Indian Army website and solve the captcha to enter the main portal.
Click on 'JCO/OR Apply/Login' for Agniveer entries or 'Officer Entry Apply/Login' for commissioned roles.
Create a new profile or log in. Ensure your profile is authenticated using your Aadhaar or Class 10th certificate.
Check the 'Eligibility' tab based on your profile details to see active recruitment rallies or exams you qualify for.
Fill out the online application form for the selected rally/exam, pay the CEE fee (if applicable), and download your admit card when notified.

title: Join Indian Navy Portal
shortDescription: Centralized recruitment platform for joining the Indian Navy as an Agniveer (SSR/MR) or a Commissioned Officer.
eligibility: Agniveer: 17.5 to 21 years with 10th (MR) or 12th with PCM (SSR). Officers: Graduate/B.Tech depending on the branch.

fees.details: ₹550 + GST for Agniveer computer-based examination.

stepsToApply: Visit the official Join Indian Navy portal and click on 'Register' located at the top right corner.
Register via Aadhaar Virtual ID or without Aadhaar by uploading your 10th passing certificate.
Log in to your dashboard using your registered email and password to complete your user profile and educational records.
Navigate to 'Current Opportunities' and select the 'Apply' button next to the active Agniveer or Officer entry scheme.
Fill in the application, upload documents, pay the examination fee online, and submit the form.

title: Indian Air Force Agniveervayu Recruitment
shortDescription: Registration portal for recruitment of unmarried male and female candidates as Agniveervayu under the Agnipath scheme.
eligibility: Unmarried candidates aged 17.5 to 21 years; 10+2 with Math/Physics/English or 3-year Diploma.

fees.details: Examination fee of ₹250 to be paid online.

requiredDocuments: Passport size recent color photograph (holding a slate with name and date)
stepsToApply: Access the official IAF Agnipath portal managed by CDAC during the active notification cycle.
Click on 'New User Register' and fill in your basic details, valid email, and mobile number to receive system-generated credentials.
Log in and proceed to fill the detailed application form, entering exact marks obtained in the qualifying examinations.
Upload the specific passport photograph (candidate holding a black slate with name and date of photo taken) along with signature and thumb impression.
Complete the online payment of ₹250, verify the application status, and print the finalized registration document.

title: RRB Centralized Employment Notice (CEN)
shortDescription: Unified platform to apply for massive government recruitment drives for NTPC, Group D, ALP, and Technical positions in Indian Railways.
eligibility: Ranges from 10th Pass / ITI for Group D/ALP to Graduate for Station Master/NTPC roles. Ages generally 18-33 (relaxable).

fees.details: ₹500 (General/OBC), ₹250 (SC/ST/Ex-SM/Female/Minorities) — partially refunded after appearing in CBT.

stepsToApply: Check the centralized notification (CEN) and navigate to the specific regional RRB portal (e.g., RRB Mumbai, RRB Allahabad).
Click on 'New Registration' and provide basic details like Aadhaar, Name, DOB, and mobile number to generate a Registration ID.
Log in and complete Part 1 of the application by filling in educational qualifications, community details, and priority list of posts.
Complete the payment of the application fee online or via bank challan.
Complete Part 2 by uploading your photograph, signature, and category certificates, then submit the final form.

title: IBPS Bank Exams Registration
shortDescription: Centralized examination portal for recruitment of Clerks, Probationary Officers (PO), and Specialist Officers across public sector banks.
eligibility: Graduation in any discipline from a recognized university. Age limits vary (usually 20-30 years for PO, 20-28 for Clerks).

fees.details: ₹850 for General/OBC/EWS; ₹175 for SC/ST/PwBD candidates.

stepsToApply: Visit the official IBPS website and click on the sliding link for the respective recruitment (CRP PO/MT, Clerks, or RRBs).
Click on 'Click here for New Registration', fill in basic contact details, and save your provisional registration number and password.
Upload your scanned photograph and signature strictly as per the specified dimensions and file sizes.
Fill in the detailed application form, entering academic scores, category details, and bank preferences.
Upload the handwritten declaration and left thumb impression, preview the entire form, process the online fee payment, and print the e-receipt.

ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
title: Aatmanirbhar Skilled Employee Employer Mapping (ASEEM)
shortDescription: An AI-based portal that maps skilled workers with available local jobs, acting as a directory of skilled workforce.
eligibility: Certified skilled candidates, migrants, and professionals seeking employment.

fees.details: Completely free registration for job seekers.

stepsToApply: Download the 'ASEEM Candidate' mobile app or visit the ASEEM web portal.
Register using your mobile number and authenticate via OTP.
Create your digital profile by adding your educational background, NSDC/PMKVY certificate ID, and work experience.
Set your location preferences and specific job roles you are seeking.
Browse suggested jobs matched by the AI engine or wait for employers to contact you directly through the platform.

department: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
title: AICTE Saksham Scholarship Scheme
shortDescription: A scholarship scheme designed to provide encouragement and financial support to specially-abled students pursuing technical education.
eligibility: Specially-abled students (disability >= 40%) admitted to 1st year of Degree/Diploma in AICTE approved institutions with family income < ₹8 Lakhs.

fees.details: No application fees.

requiredDocuments: Disability Certificate issued by competent authority
stepsToApply: Go to the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) and select the 'New Registration' or 'Login' option.
Under the 'UGC/AICTE Schemes' tab, locate the AICTE Saksham Scholarship scheme.
Fill in the demographic, academic, and bank account details accurately in the online form.
Upload digital copies of your disability certificate, income proof, and tuition fee receipts.
Submit the application form online for it to be routed to your institute's nodal officer for first-level verification.

department: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
title: AICTE PG (GATE/GPAT) Scholarship
shortDescription: Post Graduate scholarship scheme providing a monthly stipend to GATE/GPAT qualified students admitted to AICTE approved institutes.
eligibility: Students with valid GATE/GPAT score admitted as full-time scholars in AICTE approved Master of Engineering, Technology, or Pharmacy programs.

fees.details: No application fees.

requiredDocuments: Bank Account details linked with Aadhaar
stepsToApply: Contact your institute's nodal officer to ensure your student ID is generated on the AICTE portal.
Once the student ID is generated, visit the AICTE PG Scholarship portal and log in.
Validate your GATE/GPAT details, Aadhaar number, and bank account parameters on the dashboard.
Upload a scanned copy of your original scorecard and the Aadhaar seeded bank passbook.
Submit the application online. The institute will verify the attendance monthly to release the ₹12,400 stipend via Direct Benefit Transfer.

title: PG Indira Gandhi Scholarship for Single Girl Child
shortDescription: Financial assistance to single girl children pursuing a regular, full-time first-year master's degree program.
eligibility: Girls who are the only child in their family and have taken admission in regular, full-time PG programs in recognized universities. Age limit: 30 years.

fees.details: No application fee.

requiredDocuments: Affidavit regarding Single Girl Child status (₹50 Stamp Paper)
stepsToApply: Visit the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) during the active application window.
Register or log in, and navigate to the UGC schemes section to select the 'PG Indira Gandhi Scholarship'.
Complete the application form with personal, academic, and banking details.
Upload the mandatory notarized affidavit declaring single girl child status alongside admission proofs.
Submit the digital form and ensure your university's nodal officer verifies it online before the designated deadline.

title: Begum Hazrat Mahal National Scholarship
shortDescription: Scholarship for meritorious girl students belonging to minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi) studying in classes 9 to 12.
eligibility: Minority girl students studying in Classes 9th to 12th who have secured at least 50% marks in previous class; family income < ₹2.00 Lakh.

fees.details: Free to apply.

stepsToApply: Open the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) website.
Click 'New Registration' and fill out the basic student profile.
Log into the dashboard and select the 'Begum Hazrat Mahal National Scholarship' under the Ministry of Minority Affairs tab.
Fill in the academic details, select your minority community, and upload the income and community certificates.
Submit the application for school-level verification and keep tracking the status on your NSP dashboard.

title: Prime Minister's Research Fellows (PMRF)
shortDescription: Highly prestigious fellowship scheme aimed at attracting the best talent into research (Ph.D.) programs in IISc, IISERs, IITs, and select central universities.
eligibility: Students who have completed or are in final year of B.Tech/M.Tech/M.Sc from recognized institutes with high CGPA/GATE scores, admitted to a PMRF granting institute.

fees.details: No direct application fee (institutes nominate students).

stepsToApply: Secure admission into a Ph.D. program at a PMRF granting institution (IIT, IISc, IISER, NIT, or select central universities).
Apply through the 'Direct Entry Channel' during admission or 'Lateral Entry Channel' after 12-24 months of joining.
Submit a strong, innovative research proposal and your academic credentials to your institute's PMRF coordinator.
The host institute internally reviews and nominates the top candidates to the National Coordinating Institute.
Undergo the final national-level review panel interview to secure the fellowship.

title: National Digital Library of India (NDLI)
shortDescription: A virtual repository of learning resources with a single-window search facility offering millions of free educational materials.
eligibility: Open to all—students, researchers, lifelong learners, globally.

fees.details: Completely free access to most resources.

stepsToApply: Visit the NDLI website or download the NDLI mobile app.
Click on 'Register' and enter your name, email address, educational role, and preferred language.
Check your email and click the verification link sent by NDLI to activate your account.
Log in to the portal to bypass access restrictions on specialized publisher materials.
Use the search bar or browse by subject, education level, or resource type to read or download PDFs, videos, and articles.

title: Shodhganga (Reservoir of Indian Theses)
shortDescription: A digital repository of full-text doctoral theses submitted to universities in India, ensuring open access to Indian research.
eligibility: Open to anyone for reading. Ph.D. scholars from participating universities must upload their theses.

fees.details: Free to read, download, and upload.

requiredDocuments: For uploading: Approved Ph.D. Thesis in PDF format
Authentication Certificate from the University Guide
stepsToApply: To read: Visit the Shodhganga website and use the search bar to find theses by keyword, university, or researcher without needing an account.
To submit (for Ph.D. scholars): Ensure your university has signed an MoU with INFLIBNET.
Format your thesis strictly according to the Shodhganga chapter-wise PDF guidelines.
Contact your university's University Coordinator (UC) for Shodhganga.
The UC or library staff will authenticate your credentials and upload your thesis to the national repository.

title: CSIR-UGC NET Examination
shortDescription: National level exam to determine eligibility for Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) and Lectureship in Science subjects.
eligibility: Candidates possessing M.Sc. or equivalent degree/ Integrated BS-MS/BS-4 years/B.E./B.Tech/B.Pharma/MBBS with at least 55% marks.

fees.details: ₹1150 (General), ₹600 (General-EWS/OBC), ₹325 (SC/ST/Third gender).

requiredDocuments: Degree Certificate or Result Awaiting Certificate
stepsToApply: Visit the official NTA CSIR-NET portal and click on 'Online Registration'.
Generate an Application Number by providing basic demographic and contact details.
Log in and fill the application form, choosing between JRF or Assistant Professor, and selecting the subject (e.g., Life Sciences, Chemical Sciences).
Upload scanned images of your photograph, signature, and category certificate in the prescribed JPG/PDF format.
Pay the requisite fee using the integrated payment gateway and print the confirmation page for admit card generation.

title: SHREYAS (Scheme for Higher Education Youth in Apprenticeship and Skills)
shortDescription: Initiative to provide industry apprenticeship opportunities to general graduates exiting in April in non-technical programs.
eligibility: Final year students pursuing general degree courses (B.A., B.Sc., B.Com) in eligible institutions.

fees.details: No enrollment fee for students.

stepsToApply: Ensure your college or higher education institution is registered with the Sector Skill Councils under the SHREYAS mandate.
Register your interest with your college placement or skill development cell during your final year.
The college integrates skill-based add-on curricula corresponding to specific industry sectors (e.g., IT, Retail, Banking) alongside your degree.
Upon clearing the degree and the embedded skill assessment, your profile is uploaded to the NATS apprenticeship portal.
Attend the job fairs or matching sessions organized by the Sector Skill Councils to secure a stipendiary apprenticeship contract.

title: Employment News (Rojgar Samachar) Portal
shortDescription: Official weekly journal providing information on employment opportunities in Central/State Government, PSUs, and armed forces.
eligibility: Anyone looking for verified government job notifications.

fees.details: Annual e-version subscription costs ₹400; print version subscription costs ₹530.

requiredDocuments: None required to browse free alerts. Email ID required for e-subscription.
stepsToApply: Visit the official Employment News website.
To subscribe to the e-version, click on the 'e-Version' link and create a user account.
Fill in your billing details and select the 1-year, 2-year, or 3-year subscription plan.
Make the online payment through Bharatkosh or the integrated payment gateway.
Log in every Saturday to download and read the authenticated digital PDF of upcoming government vacancies and results.

ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
department: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment
title: National Fellowship for SC Students (NFSC)
shortDescription: Fellowship scheme to provide financial assistance to Scheduled Caste students pursuing M.Phil and Ph.D. degrees in Sciences, Humanities, and Social Sciences.
eligibility: SC candidates who have qualified UGC-NET or CSIR-NET and are admitted to M.Phil/Ph.D. programs.

fees.details: Selection based on NET scores; no separate application fee.

stepsToApply: Appear for and clear the UGC-NET or CSIR-NET examination conducted by NTA.
When registering for NET, ensure you correctly mark your category as 'SC' and opt for the fellowship consideration.
NTA and the Ministry generate a merit list of eligible SC candidates based on NET scores independently.
If selected, download your official NFSC award letter from the designated UGC/Ministry portal.
Submit the award letter along with the joining report to your university's research cell to link your bank account to the Canara Bank scholar portal for DBT.

ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
title: Jan Shikshan Sansthan Vocational Training
shortDescription: Vocational skill training programs targeting non-literates, neo-literates, and school dropouts at their doorsteps in rural and urban slums.
eligibility: Non-literates, neo-literates, persons with rudimentary education up to 8th standard, and school dropouts up to 12th standard in age group 15-45.

fees.details: Nominal course fee (often exempted for SC/ST, women, and BPL candidates).

requiredDocuments: BPL Card or Category Certificate (for fee exemption)
stepsToApply: Locate the nearest Jan Shikshan Sansthan (JSS) NGO partner office in your district using the JSS portal directory.
Visit the center and consult the program officer regarding upcoming short-term courses (e.g., tailoring, plumbing, beauty wellness).
Fill out the physical enrollment form provided at the center and submit your identity documents.
Attend the practical training sessions conducted in your local village or slum community center.
Take the final assessment conducted by the National Institute of Open Schooling (NIOS) or SSCs to receive your recognized certificate.

title: Atal Innovation Mission Programs
shortDescription: Flagship initiative to promote a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship across schools, universities, and industries.
eligibility: Schools (for Atal Tinkering Labs), Universities/Institutions (for Atal Incubation Centres), and Student Innovators.

fees.details: Grant-based programs. No fee to apply for grants or participation.

stepsToApply: To set up an ATL in a school, the principal must apply via the AIM portal during the application window by submitting the school's infrastructure details.
For students wanting to participate in innovation challenges, watch out for the ATL Marathon or Tinkerpreneur announcements on the AIM website.
Form a team, develop a prototype or business idea, and submit a video pitch/document on the designated challenge portal.
If selected, receive mentoring from 'Mentors of Change' and potential funding/incubation support to scale the idea.

title: Swayam Prabha DTH Channels
shortDescription: A group of DTH channels devoted to telecasting high-quality educational programs on a 24x7 basis across the country.
eligibility: Anyone with a television and a DD Free Dish or DishTV setup.

fees.details: Free to air on DD Free Dish.

stepsToApply: Install a DD Free Dish Set-Top Box and antenna at your home.
Scan the satellite channels on your television.
Locate the Swayam Prabha channels (channels vary by educational level: higher ed, school ed, IIT-PAL).
Visit the Swayam Prabha website to download the weekly broadcast schedule for your specific class or subject.
Tune in at the scheduled time to watch the lectures, which are repeated several times a day for convenience.

title: e-PG Pathshala
shortDescription: An initiative providing high-quality, curriculum-based, interactive e-content in various postgraduate subjects.
eligibility: Postgraduate students, teachers, and researchers.

fees.details: Completely free open educational resource.

stepsToApply: Open the e-PG Pathshala portal on any web browser.
Select your broad subject category from the homepage (e.g., Social Sciences, Arts/Humanities, Basic Sciences).
Choose the specific paper/module you wish to study from the dropdown list.
Access the learning material, which is divided into four quadrants: e-Text, Self-Learning Video, Learn More (web resources), and Self-Assessment.
Read, watch, or download the modules without the need for registration or logins.

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
title: UPSC Online Recruitment Application (ORA)
shortDescription: Portal for applying to various direct recruitment selection posts (Specialists, Medical Officers, Assistant Directors) across central ministries.
eligibility: Highly dependent on the specific post (often requires specialized Master's degrees, Ph.Ds, or specific work experience).

fees.details: ₹25 (Exempted for SC/ST/PwBD/Women).

stepsToApply: Monitor the UPSC website for specific Direct Recruitment Notifications (ORA).
Go to the UPSC ORA portal and read the specific advertisement details for the post you are targeting.
Click on 'Apply Now', log in using your UPSC OTR (One Time Registration) credentials.
Fill in the exhaustive post-specific module, paying special attention to entering your exact dates of work experience, as this determines shortlisting.
Upload all supporting experience and educational certificates in PDF format, pay the ₹25 fee online, and submit.

title: UGC Ishan Uday Scholarship
shortDescription: Special scholarship scheme for students of the North Eastern Region (NER) to promote higher education.
eligibility: Students domiciled in NER who have passed Class XII and secured admission in a general degree/technical/professional course. Family income must not exceed ₹4.5 Lakhs per annum.

fees.details: No application fees.

stepsToApply: Visit the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) and click on 'New Registration' or 'Login' if already registered.
Navigate to the 'UGC/AICTE Schemes' tab and select 'Ishan Uday Special Scholarship Scheme for NER'.
Fill in your personal, academic, and bank account details accurately.
Upload scanned copies of your domicile certificate, family income certificate, and Aadhaar card.
Submit the application online. Ensure you track the application status until it is verified by your college/university nodal officer.

title: Central Sector Scholarship Scheme
shortDescription: Financial assistance for meritorious college and university students from low-income families to meet day-to-day expenses.
eligibility: Students above the 80th percentile of successful candidates in the relevant stream in Class XII, with a family income of less than ₹4.5 Lakhs per annum.

fees.details: No application fee.

stepsToApply: Log into the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) using your credentials.
Select 'Central Sector Scheme of Scholarships for College and University Students' under the Department of Higher Education section.
Enter your academic scores, board details, and institutional admission details.
Upload the mandatory income certificate and other identity proofs in the specified digital format.
Submit the form for institutional verification. The scholarship amount (₹12,000 to ₹20,000 per annum) will be credited via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

department: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
title: AICTE Swanath Scholarship
shortDescription: Financial support of ₹50,000 per annum for orphans, wards of martyred armed forces, or COVID-19 impacted students pursuing technical education.
eligibility: Orphans, wards of parents who died due to COVID-19, or wards of Armed Forces/Paramilitary personnel martyred in action. Must be studying in an AICTE approved institution.

fees.details: Zero application cost.

requiredDocuments: Admission letter from AICTE approved institute
stepsToApply: Access the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) and navigate to the UGC/AICTE Schemes section.
Select the 'AICTE Swanath Scholarship Scheme' option.
Complete the application form, ensuring you correctly select your qualifying category (Orphan/COVID-19/Martyr).
Upload the respective supporting documents issued by competent authorities (e.g., SDM/Tehsildar/Armed Forces HQ).
Submit the application to your institute for the first level of digital verification on the NSP portal.

title: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Minorities
shortDescription: Scholarship for minority community students (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi) studying in classes 1 to 10.
eligibility: Minority students studying in classes 1 to 10 securing at least 50% marks in the previous final examination. Family income must not exceed ₹1 Lakh per annum.

fees.details: Free application process.

stepsToApply: Visit the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) and click 'New Registration'.
Select the 'Pre-Matric Scholarship Schemes Minorities' option under the Ministry of Minority Affairs.
Enter the student's demographic details, religion, and previous year's academic scores.
Upload the self-certified community certificate and parent's income proof.
Submit the application. School nodal officers will verify the credentials on their portal to process the application further.

ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
department: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment
title: Top Class Education Scheme for SC Students
shortDescription: Full financial support for SC students to pursue degree/post-degree courses in notified institutes of excellence like IITs, IIMs, and NITs.
eligibility: SC students who secure admission in notified institutions. Total family income must not exceed ₹8 Lakhs per annum.

fees.details: No application fees.

requiredDocuments: Admission proof from the notified institute
stepsToApply: Confirm that your admitted institution is on the Ministry's notified list of 'Top Class' institutes.
Log into the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) and locate the 'Top Class Education Scheme for SC Students'.
Fill in the digital application with your JEE/CAT/NEET rank and admission details.
Upload your caste certificate, income proof, and the institutional fee structure document.
Submit the application online. The scholarship covers full tuition fees, living expenses, and books, disbursed directly via DBT.

ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
department: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment
title: National Overseas Scholarship for SC
shortDescription: Financial assistance to selected SC, Denotified Nomadic Tribes, and Landless Agricultural Labourer students for pursuing Masters or Ph.D. courses abroad.
eligibility: Students belonging to specified categories who have secured an unconditional offer of admission from a top 500 QS-ranked foreign university. Family income must be below ₹8 Lakhs.

fees.details: Zero application processing fee.

stepsToApply: Obtain an unconditional admission offer from a recognized foreign university (Top 500 QS ranking).
Visit the dedicated NOS-MSJE portal and register a new user profile.
Fill out the comprehensive application detailing the foreign university, course duration, and expected tuition fees.
Upload all statutory documents, including visa (if obtained), passport, caste certificate, and academic transcripts.
Submit the application during the active phase. Selected candidates will be issued an Award Letter to facilitate visa processing and fee disbursement.

title: India Post GDS Recruitment
shortDescription: Direct recruitment portal for Gramin Dak Sevaks (GDS), Branch Postmasters (BPM), and Assistant Branch Postmasters (ABPM) based on 10th-grade marks.
eligibility: Candidates aged 18 to 40 years who have passed the 10th standard with passing marks in Mathematics and English.

fees.details: ₹100 for UR/OBC/EWS male candidates. Free for all female, SC, ST, and PwD candidates.

stepsToApply: Visit the India Post GDS Online Engagement portal and click on 'Stage 1: Registration'.
Validate your mobile number and email via OTP, then fill in your basic details and Class 10 passing year/board.
Proceed to 'Stage 2: Apply Online', log in with your Registration Number, and fill in your address and exact marks obtained in each 10th-grade subject.
Choose your preferences (Division and Post Office branches) where you wish to apply.
Pay the ₹100 fee online (if applicable) and submit the final form. Selection is automated purely based on merit (10th marks).

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
title: SSC GD Constable Recruitment
shortDescription: Massive annual recruitment drive for General Duty Constables in CAPFs (BSF, CISF, CRPF, ITBP, SSB, Assam Rifles, SSF).
eligibility: Candidates aged 18 to 23 years who have passed Matriculation (10th Class) from a recognized board.

fees.details: ₹100 application fee. Exempt for Women, SC, ST, and Ex-Servicemen.

stepsToApply: Log into the new SSC portal (ssc.gov.in) using your One-Time Registration (OTR) credentials.
Navigate to the 'Latest Notifications' section and click 'Apply' under the Constable (GD) examination card.
Verify your pre-filled OTR data, select your examination center preferences, and indicate your state/district of domicile.
Provide your preference order for the various CAPF forces (e.g., A for BSF, B for CISF).
Upload your photo and signature, accept the declaration, pay the ₹100 fee online, and download the application receipt.

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
title: SSC Junior Engineer (JE) Recruitment
shortDescription: Recruitment examination for Junior Engineers (Civil, Mechanical, Electrical) in various central government organizations like CPWD and MES.
eligibility: Degree or Diploma in Civil/Electrical/Mechanical Engineering. Age limit typically up to 30 or 32 years depending on the department.

fees.details: ₹100 application fee. Exempt for Women, SC, ST, PwD, and Ex-Servicemen.

stepsToApply: Access the SSC portal and log in via your OTR (One Time Registration) ID.
Click on 'Apply' under the Junior Engineer (Civil, Mechanical & Electrical) Examination notification.
Fill out the supplementary details, select your specific engineering stream, and choose three examination centers in your preferred region.
Upload the required standard dimensions of your photograph and signature.
Complete the online payment gateway process, submit the application, and retain a printout for future document verification phases.

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
title: UPSC NDA & NA Examination
shortDescription: Biannual entrance examination for admission to the Army, Navy, and Air Force wings of the National Defence Academy.
eligibility: Unmarried male and female candidates aged 16.5 to 19.5 years. 12th pass for Army; 12th pass with PCM for Navy/Air Force.

fees.details: ₹100. Female, SC, and ST candidates are exempted from payment.

stepsToApply: Visit the UPSC Online portal and log in using your OTR (One Time Registration) credentials.
Click on 'Latest Notifications' and select 'Apply' for the National Defence Academy and Naval Academy Examination.
Complete Part I of the application: Update educational qualifications, select your branch preferences (Army/Navy/Air Force), and choose your exam center.
Complete Part II: Pay the ₹100 examination fee (if applicable) and upload your photograph, signature, and a PDF of your Photo ID.
Review the declaration, click 'I Agree', and download the finalized application form.

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
title: UPSC Combined Defence Services (CDS)
shortDescription: Examination for recruitment into the Indian Military Academy, Naval Academy, Air Force Academy, and Officers Training Academy.
eligibility: Unmarried graduates (age 19-24 for IMA/INA, 20-24 for AFA, 19-25 for OTA). Engineering degree required for INA/AFA.

fees.details: ₹200. Female, SC, and ST candidates are exempted from payment.

stepsToApply: Log into the UPSC OTR portal and navigate to the CDS Examination notification.
Proceed to Part I registration, verify your basic details, and select your specific academy preferences based on your degree qualification.
Move to Part II registration to remit the ₹200 fee securely via SBI net banking, UPI, or card.
Upload standard compliant images of your photograph and signature, along with your Photo ID document.
Select your examination center, agree to the final terms, and submit the application.

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
title: UPSC Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) AC
shortDescription: Direct recruitment examination for the post of Assistant Commandants (Officers) in CAPFs (BSF, CRPF, CISF, ITBP, SSB).
eligibility: Male and female Indian citizens aged 20 to 25 years holding a Bachelor's degree from a recognized university.

fees.details: ₹200. Female, SC, and ST candidates are exempted from payment.

stepsToApply: Access the UPSC OTR dashboard and locate the Central Armed Police Forces (ACs) Examination link.
Fill out the Part I form, detailing your educational background, NCC certification status, and community category.
In Part II, pay the ₹200 application fee through the online payment gateway.
Upload your scanned photograph, signature, and valid identity card PDF.
Select your preferred examination city, submit the form, and prepare for the physical and medical efficiency tests following the written exam.

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
title: UPSC Engineering Services Examination (ESE)
shortDescription: National level exam to recruit engineers for Class-1 officer posts in various technical departments of the Government of India.
eligibility: Degree in Engineering (Civil/Mechanical/Electrical/Electronics & Telecom). Age limit: 21 to 30 years.

fees.details: ₹200. Female, SC, ST, and PwBD candidates are exempted.

stepsToApply: Log into your UPSC OTR account and click to apply for the Engineering Services Examination.
During Part I registration, verify your demographic data, select your specific engineering discipline, and choose the Preliminary exam center.
Proceed to Part II, pay the application fee of ₹200 via online banking or cards.
Upload your scanned images and the PDF of your government-issued Photo ID.
Confirm the final submission. If you clear the Prelims, you will be required to fill out a Detailed Application Form (DAF) for the Mains.

title: Indian Coast Guard Navik/Yantrik Recruitment
shortDescription: Join the Indian Coast Guard as Navik (General Duty), Navik (Domestic Branch), or Yantrik through the centralized CGEPT portal.
eligibility: Male Indian citizens aged 18-22. 10th pass for DB; 12th pass with Math/Physics for GD; Diploma in Electrical/Mechanical for Yantrik.

fees.details: ₹300. SC/ST candidates are exempted from payment.

requiredDocuments: Scanned Photograph (holding a slate with name & date)
stepsToApply: Visit the ICG CGEPT portal and click on 'Create Account' using your email ID and mobile number.
Log in with the system-generated password, change it, and fill out your personal and educational profile.
Select the specific post (Navik GD, Navik DB, or Yantrik) based on your educational eligibility.
Upload your specialized photograph (holding a black slate with your name and photo date written in chalk), signature, and thumb impression.
Pay the ₹300 exam fee online, select your city preferences for Stage I testing, and print the final application.

title: SBI Probationary Officer (PO) Recruitment
shortDescription: Highly competitive annual recruitment examination to hire Probationary Officers in State Bank of India branches nationwide.
eligibility: Graduates in any discipline from a recognized university. Age limit: 21 to 30 years.

fees.details: ₹750 for General/EWS/OBC candidates. Nil for SC/ST/PwBD candidates.

stepsToApply: Visit the SBI Careers page, click on 'Current Openings', and select the SBI PO advertisement.
Click 'Apply Online' which redirects to the IBPS portal. Click 'New Registration' to get your registration number.
Fill in your basic details, upload your scanned photograph and signature, and enter your academic qualifications.
Upload the specific handwritten declaration (in English) and your left thumb impression.
Preview your application, proceed to the payment gateway to pay the ₹750 fee, and download the final e-receipt.

title: RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment
shortDescription: Direct recruitment for Officers in Grade 'B' (General, DEPR, DSIM) in the Reserve Bank of India.
eligibility: Graduates with 60% marks (50% for SC/ST/PwBD) or Postgraduates with 55% marks. Age limit: 21 to 30 years.

fees.details: ₹850 for General/OBC/EWS; ₹100 (Intimation charges only) for SC/ST/PwBD.

stepsToApply: Access the RBI Opportunities portal, navigate to 'Current Vacancies' -> 'Vacancies', and click on the Grade B notification.
Click on the 'Online Application' link, register to generate a provisional ID, and log in.
Enter your detailed academic credentials, ensuring you meet the strict percentage criteria.
Upload your photo, signature, thumb impression, and the prescribed handwritten declaration text.
Pay the ₹850 application fee securely online and retain the confirmation page for the Phase-I examination.

title: LIC Assistant Administrative Officer (AAO)
shortDescription: National level exam to recruit Assistant Administrative Officers (Generalist/IT/CA/Actuarial) in LIC.
eligibility: Bachelor’s Degree in any discipline (for Generalist) or specific professional degrees (for IT/CA). Age limit: 21 to 30 years.

fees.details: ₹700 + GST for General/OBC/EWS; ₹85 + GST for SC/ST/PwBD.

stepsToApply: Visit the LIC India Careers page and click on the specific 'Recruitment of AAO' link.
Click 'Apply Online' and complete the new registration process.
Fill in your personal, educational, and experience details in the respective tabs.
Upload the required documents, ensuring the signature is not in capital letters and the handwritten declaration matches the exact text in the notification.
Pay the ₹700 fee (+ transaction charges) online, submit the form, and download the printout.

ministry: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution
title: FCI Category III Recruitment
shortDescription: Recruitment drive for Assistant Grade III (General, Accounts, Technical, Depot), Junior Engineer, and Steno roles in FCI.
eligibility: Graduate Degree with computer proficiency (for AG-III); Engineering Degree/Diploma for JE. Age limits typically 27 or 28 years.

fees.details: ₹500 for UR/OBC/EWS. Exempt for SC/ST/PwBD and Women candidates.

stepsToApply: Visit the FCI official website, navigate to 'Current Recruitment', and select the Category III advertisement.
Choose the Zone (North, South, East, West, North-East) you wish to apply for. Note: You can apply for only one zone.
Register on the application portal, fill out your demographic and educational details, and select the specific post (e.g., AG-III Depot).
Upload the scanned images (photo, signature, thumb impression, declaration).
Pay the ₹500 examination fee via the online payment gateway and print the final application form.

title: ICAR AIEEA (UG/PG) Examination
shortDescription: All India Entrance Examination for Admission to Bachelor and Master degree programs in Agriculture and Allied Sciences.
eligibility: For UG: 10+2 with PCB/PCM/PCA. For PG: Bachelor's degree in respective agriculture disciplines.

fees.details: Approx. ₹800 (UG General); ₹1160 (PG General). Reduced fees for SC/ST/PwD.

stepsToApply: Visit the official NTA ICAR portal during the active application period.
Click on the registration link for either AIEEA (UG) or AIEEA (PG) and create an application number.
Fill out the detailed application form, selecting your preferred subject group and examination cities.
Upload standard scanned images of your photograph, signature, and thumb impression.
Process the examination fee payment online using SBI/Canara/HDFC payment gateways and save the confirmation page.

title: CMAT (Common Management Admission Test)
shortDescription: National level entrance examination for admission to management programs (MBA/PGDM) in AICTE-approved institutions.
eligibility: Graduates in any discipline. Final year students of graduate courses (10+2+3) are also eligible to apply.

fees.details: ₹2000 for Male (General/OBC); ₹1000 for Female/SC/ST/PwD/Third Gender.

stepsToApply: Visit the NTA CMAT official website and click on 'Online Registration'.
Register using an email ID and mobile number to generate your unique Application Number.
Log in and fill out the detailed application, providing academic qualifications and choosing up to four exam city preferences.
Upload a clear, recent passport-size photograph (with 80% face coverage) and your signature in JPG/JPEG format.
Pay the examination fee online. Upon successful transaction, download and securely save the Confirmation Page.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
department: National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences
title: NEET-PG Registration
shortDescription: Single eligibility cum entrance examination for admission to MD/MS and PG Diploma Courses.
eligibility: Candidates possessing an MBBS degree/Provisional Pass Certificate and having completed one year of internship.

fees.details: ₹3500 for General/OBC/EWS; ₹2500 for SC/ST/PwD candidates.

requiredDocuments: Scanned Photograph, Signature, and Thumb Impression
stepsToApply: Go to the NBEMS official portal and click on the 'NEET-PG' examination link.
Complete the initial registration to receive a User ID and Password on your registered email and mobile.
Fill out the comprehensive application form, detailing your MBBS college, internship dates, and medical council registration number.
Upload strictly formatted images of your photograph, signature, and thumb impression (failing strict NBE image guidelines leads to rejection).
Select your test city, pay the examination fee online, and print the application summary.

title: CUET (PG) Registration
shortDescription: Common University Entrance Test providing a single-window opportunity for postgraduate admissions across Central and State Universities.
eligibility: Candidates who have passed a Bachelor's degree or equivalent, or are appearing in the final year of graduation.

fees.details: Base fee (up to 2 test papers): ₹1200 (General), ₹1000 (OBC/EWS), ₹900 (SC/ST). Additional fees per extra paper.

stepsToApply: Visit the CUET PG Samarth portal and click on 'Register' to create a new application profile.
Fill in your personal details and generate a secure password.
Log in and carefully select the Universities and specific PG courses/test paper codes you wish to apply for.
Upload the mandatory scanned images in the prescribed format.
Pay the cumulative examination fee based on the number of test papers selected, verify the payment status, and download the confirmation page.

department: National Council for Hotel Management & Catering Technology
title: NCHM JEE Registration
shortDescription: National level entrance exam conducted by NTA for admission to the B.Sc. (Hospitality & Hotel Administration) program.
eligibility: Candidates who have passed 10+2 system of Senior Secondary examination or its equivalent with English as one of the subjects.

fees.details: ₹1000 (General/OBC); ₹450 (SC/ST/PwD/Transgender).

stepsToApply: Access the NCHM JEE NTA portal and select 'New Registration'.
Read the information bulletin and submit your basic details to generate an Application Number.
Complete the application form by entering your academic details and selecting examination cities.
Upload your scanned photograph and signature in JPG format.
Pay the required application fee online via integrated gateways and download the confirmation page for your records.

title: NIFT Entrance Examination
shortDescription: National level entrance exam (conducted by NTA) for admission to Bachelor’s and Master’s programs in Design and Fashion Technology.
eligibility: For B.Des/B.F.Tech: 10+2 passed/appearing. Max age 24 years. For Masters: Valid undergraduate degree. No age limit.

fees.details: ₹3000 for General/OBC/EWS; ₹1500 for SC/ST/PwD candidates.

stepsToApply: Visit the NIFT admission portal (now managed by NTA) during the registration window.
Create an account to obtain an application ID and password.
Fill out the detailed application form, selecting the specific program (B.Des, B.F.Tech, M.Des, etc.) and preferred test cities.
Upload scanned copies of your signature, photograph, and caste certificate (if applicable).
Pay the examination fee online and print the submitted application form.

title: NID Design Aptitude Test (DAT)
shortDescription: Entrance examination for admission to B.Des and M.Des programs at National Institutes of Design across India.
eligibility: B.Des: Passed/appearing in 10+2. Age limit up to 20 years. M.Des: Bachelor's degree of 3/4 years. Age limit up to 30 years.

fees.details: ₹3000 for General/OBC/EWS; ₹1500 for SC/ST/PwD (per discipline for M.Des).

stepsToApply: Sign up on the official NID admissions portal by providing a valid email address and mobile number.
Log in to your account and fill in your personal, educational, and test center preference details.
Upload your scanned photograph and signature strictly as per the specified formats.
If applying for M.Des, select your specific disciplines (up to two).
Pay the application fee online. Keep a copy of the payment receipt and the submitted application form.

title: CLAT (Common Law Admission Test)
shortDescription: National level entrance exam for admissions to undergraduate (UG) and postgraduate (PG) law programs offered by 24 NLUs.
eligibility: UG: 10+2 with 45% marks (40% for SC/ST). PG: LLB degree with 50% marks. No upper age limit.

fees.details: ₹4000 for General/OBC/PWD/NRI; ₹3500 for SC/ST/BPL candidates.

stepsToApply: Visit the Consortium of NLUs website and click on 'Register' to create an account using your mobile number and email ID.
Log in, select the program (UG or PG), and fill out the extensive application form.
Indicate your preference order for all 24 participating National Law Universities.
Upload the required documents, including your photograph, signature, and state domicile certificate if claiming regional quotas.
Pay the application fee online, submit the form, and download the acknowledgement.

title: CAT (Common Admission Test)
shortDescription: Premier national-level management aptitude test for admission to MBA/PGP programs at IIMs and other top B-schools.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree with at least 50% marks or equivalent CGPA (45% for SC, ST, and PwD candidates).

fees.details: ₹2400 for General/EWS/NC-OBC; ₹1200 for SC/ST/PwD candidates.

requiredDocuments: Passport size Photograph (white background) and Signature
stepsToApply: Access the official CAT website and click on 'Register' to generate a User ID and Password.
Log in with the credentials received on your email/SMS and fill out the detailed application form.
Upload the mandatory documents, ensuring the photograph is recent and has a clear white background.
Select your preferred IIM programs and the corresponding interview cities.
Select your test city preferences, pay the non-refundable registration fee online, and print the application summary.

title: IIT JAM Examination
shortDescription: Joint Admission test for Masters for admission to M.Sc., Joint M.Sc.-Ph.D., and other PG programs at IITs, IISc, and NITs.
eligibility: Candidates who have completed an undergraduate degree or are currently studying in their final year. Foreign nationals are also eligible.

fees.details: ₹900 for Female/SC/ST/PwD and ₹1800 for All Others (for 1 test paper).

stepsToApply: Access the JOAPS (JAM Online Application Processing System) portal.
Register using your name, valid email ID, and mobile number to receive an Enrollment ID.
Log in, fill out the personal and academic details, and select up to two test papers (e.g., Physics, Biotechnology).
Upload your photograph, signature, and Category/PwD certificates ensuring strict adherence to the specified file sizes.
Pay the application fee online, verify the application status, and download the submitted form.

title: UCEED & CEED Registration
shortDescription: Undergraduate and Common Entrance Exams for Design for admission to B.Des and M.Des programs at IITs and IISc.
eligibility: UCEED: 10+2 passed/appearing. Max age limit applies. CEED: Bachelor's degree/diploma in Design/Architecture/Fine Arts/Engineering.

fees.details: ₹1900 for Female/SC/ST/PwD candidates; ₹3800 for all other candidates.

requiredDocuments: Qualifying Exam Certificate / Certificate from College Principal
stepsToApply: Visit the official UCEED or CEED registration portal depending on your targeted degree level.
Register using a valid email ID and complete the email verification process.
Fill in your personal, educational, and contact details, and choose your preferred examination cities.
Upload the required documents, ensuring the 'Certificate from Principal' is attached if you are in your final year.
Pay the registration fee online via billdesk, verify the successful transaction, and download the registration receipt.

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
title: SSC CGL (Combined Graduate Level)
shortDescription: Premier national recruitment exam for various Group B and Group C officer posts in ministries and departments of the Govt. of India.
eligibility: Bachelor’s Degree from a recognized University. Age limits vary by post (usually 18-27, 20-30, or 18-32 years).

fees.details: ₹100 application fee. Completely exempt for Women, SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen.

stepsToApply: Log into the main SSC portal using your OTR (One Time Registration) ID and password.
Under 'Latest Notifications', locate the Combined Graduate Level Examination and click 'Apply'.
Review your auto-filled demographic details, select your preferred examination centers, and indicate if you are applying for specialized posts (like Junior Statistical Officer).
Upload your live or scanned photograph (as per the latest SSC notification rules) and your signature.
Pay the ₹100 fee online, submit the form before the server deadline, and print the confirmation for your records.
